w:r.w:t: Prepared from the uploaded function outline. Screen mock-up placeholders should be replaced with actual UI screenshots/prototypes from the project source.
Guest passenger or operator representative opens the registration screen and submits required account/company information.
Allow a new passenger account or operator onboarding request to be created and verified by email.
Registration screen / operator registration web form

wp:docPr.name: VietRide UI prototype 1
wp:docPr.descr: Registration screen / operator registration web form

pic:cNvPr.name: ui_1.png
pic:cNvPr.descr: Registration screen / operator registration web form

w:r.w:t: Name, email, phone, password, confirmation code; for operator: company profile, tax code, business registration, representative contact.
Required fields cannot be blank; email format must be valid; password must match policy; company identifiers must not duplicate existing operators.
Registration requires email verification. Operator registration remains pending until System Admin approval.
User submits registration information, system validates it, sends OTP, verifies email, and creates a usable or pending account.
Duplicated email/company code, expired OTP, invalid password, or missing required information returns an error and asks the user to correct data.
User clicks Login button from the relevant mobile app or web portal.
Passenger, Driver, Assistant Staff, Operator Staff, Operator Admin, System Admin
Authenticate users and route them to the correct application or dashboard according to role.
Email/username and password; optional device information.
Account must exist, password must be correct, account must not be locked, and role must match the target app/portal.
Only approved operator accounts can access the operator dashboard. Staff accounts must complete initial password setup before login.
System validates credentials, creates session/token, loads role permissions, and redirects to the correct home page.
Invalid credentials, locked account, unapproved operator, or inactive staff account shows a clear error message.
User selects Forgot Password from login screen.
Allow users to recover access through email verification and password reset.
Registered email, OTP/reset token, new password, confirm password.
Email must exist; OTP/reset token must be valid and not expired; new password must satisfy password policy.
Reset token is single-use and time-limited.
System sends recovery email, verifies token, updates password, and invalidates previous reset token.
Unknown email, expired token, weak password, or mismatch password blocks reset.
Staff user opens invitation link sent after account creation.
Driver, Assistant Staff, Operator Staff, Operator Admin created by admin
Allow staff accounts created by admin to set their first password securely.
Invitation token, new password, confirm password.
Token must be valid, not expired, and match the created staff account; password must satisfy policy.
Invitation link is time-limited and single-use.
User opens link, sets password, and account becomes active for login.
Expired or used token requires admin to resend invitation.
Authenticated user clicks Logout from app/menu/profile.
Securely end the current login session.
Refresh/session token is invalidated where applicable.
System clears local session and redirects user to login screen.
If network fails, app clears local session and retries server-side invalidation later if needed.
Passenger enters departure, arrival, and date on search screen.
Find available trips that match travel needs.
Departure location, arrival location, travel date, filters such as time/operator/price.
Locations and date are required; date cannot be invalid; filters must be within supported values.
Only published and bookable trips are shown.
System returns matching trips with schedule, operator, fare, and available seats summary.
No result shows empty state and suggests changing filters.
Passenger selects a trip from search results.
Show detailed route, schedule, operator, stops, fare, and seat availability.
Trip must exist and be visible/bookable.
Seat availability may change until booking is locked.
System displays trip details and seat map.
Unavailable or expired trip returns message and prompts passenger to search again.
Passenger selects seat(s), pickup/drop-off points, and confirms booking information.
Create a booking request before payment.
Trip, selected seats, pickup/drop-off stop, shuttle option if supported.
Seat count cannot exceed allowed maximum; pickup/drop-off must belong to trip route; selected seats must be available.
Seats are locked for a limited time during checkout.
System validates selection, locks seats, creates pending booking, and moves to payment.
Seat conflict or timeout releases seats and asks passenger to select again.
Passenger enters or selects voucher during checkout.
Apply eligible platform voucher to reduce payable amount.
Voucher code, booking amount, trip/operator context.
Voucher must be active, within validity period, and applicable to the trip/operator/user.
Usage limits and minimum order rules apply.
System validates voucher and recalculates total amount.
Invalid, expired, inapplicable, or fully used voucher returns clear error.
Passenger confirms payment by Wallet or payment gateway.
Complete ticket payment and confirm booking.
Booking ID, payment method, payable amount.
Booking must be pending payment; payment amount must match current checkout total.
Unpaid booking expires after payment timeout.
Successful payment confirms booking and issues e-ticket/QR code.
Failed or timeout payment expires booking and releases seats.
View ticket list, status, detail, and QR code.
Passenger can only view own bookings.
System displays upcoming, completed, cancelled, and refunded tickets.
If no ticket exists, system displays an empty state.
Passenger opens tracking for an eligible confirmed trip.
Track bus GPS location and ETA.
Trip must be eligible for tracking and passenger must have access.
Tracking is available during configured trip stages.
System shows bus location, route, stop ETA, and status updates.
If GPS is unavailable, show last known location or unavailable message.
Passenger selects edit pickup/drop-off from ticket detail before cutoff.
Update eligible pickup or drop-off point.
Booking ID, new pickup stop, new drop-off stop.
New stops must belong to route and satisfy order rules; cutoff time must not be exceeded.
Editing is blocked after allowed cutoff or after trip begins.
System updates booking stop information and notifies passenger.
Invalid stop, expired cutoff, or trip status conflict returns error.
Passenger clicks Cancel Ticket from ticket detail.
Cancel eligible ticket and request refund according to policy.
Booking must be cancellable and trip must not be in restricted status.
Refund amount is calculated by operator cancellation policy.
System shows preview, cancels ticket, releases seat, refunds wallet, and notifies passenger.
If not eligible, system shows cancellation not allowed reason.
Passenger opens trip change notification or ticket detail.
Accept or reject operator schedule/route change.
Action must be active and belong to passenger booking.
Reject may trigger refund according to change severity/policy.
Passenger accepts to keep booking or rejects to cancel/refund.
Expired action is handled by system fallback policy.
Passenger opens parcel delivery trip selection.
View trips that support parcel delivery.
Origin, destination, date, parcel service filter.
Trip must support parcel capacity and parcel service.
System lists trips available for parcel delivery.
No available trip shows empty state.
Passenger enters parcel delivery information and submits request.
Create a parcel request with sender, recipient, route and parcel data.
Required fields cannot be blank; parcel size/weight must be within allowed values; prohibited items are not allowed.
Extra-large parcels may require operator review before payment.
System validates request, calculates or requests fee approval, and creates pending parcel request.
Capacity exceeded, invalid information, or rejected review stops request.
3.5.3 View Parcel Detail and Review Status
Passenger opens parcel detail from parcel list.
View parcel information and processing status.
User must have permission to view parcel.
Status is updated by payment, loading, transit, unloading, and confirmation actions.
System displays parcel status timeline and relevant actions.
Unauthorized access or invalid parcel shows error.
Passenger proceeds to pay required parcel fee.
Parcel request ID, amount, payment method.
Parcel must be pending payment and amount must match required fee.
Payment timeout expires unpaid parcel request.
Successful payment moves parcel to operational processing.
Failed or timeout payment marks parcel as expired.
Sender or eligible recipient opens parcel tracking.
Track parcel delivery status and trip movement where eligible.
Parcel tracking token or parcel ID.
User must be sender, eligible recipient, or have valid tracking link.
Tracking availability depends on trip status.
System shows current parcel status and related trip movement.
Expired or invalid token blocks access.
Recipient opens delivery confirmation link or in-app action.
Confirm receipt or reject parcel delivery.
Token/action must be valid and not expired.
Reject may start return process after undo window.
Recipient confirms receipt to complete delivery or rejects to start exception process.
Expired link or invalid action shows error.
View wallet balance and available wallet actions.
Wallet balance is read-only except through approved transactions.
System displays balance, top-up action, and recent transactions.
If wallet not initialized, system initializes or shows unavailable message.
Passenger clicks Top Up and enters amount/payment method.
Create and pay a wallet top-up request.
Amount must be within allowed limits; payment method must be supported.
Successful payment increases wallet balance and records transaction.
Failed or timeout payment does not change wallet balance.
View wallet transaction list and details.
Passenger can only view own wallet transactions.
System lists top-up, payment, refund and adjustment records.
Empty state appears if no transaction exists.
View payment records for tickets, parcels and wallet.
Only user-owned payment records are visible.
System shows payment status, amount, method and related order.
Failed data loading shows retry option.
View assigned trips and work schedule.
Authenticated driver account and date range.
Driver must be active and assigned to operator.
System displays upcoming and past assignments.
View operational details of an assigned trip.
Driver must be assigned to trip.
Trip details are read-only except allowed operation actions.
System displays route, vehicle, stops, passenger summary and timing.
Driver opens route tab for assigned trip.
Trip must be assigned to driver.
Stops are shown in route order.
System displays route map/list and next stop information.
Missing route data shows operation alert.
3.7.4 Manage Trip Status and Broadcast GPS
Driver starts trip operation and enables GPS.
Update trip status and broadcast location during active trip.
Trip ID, GPS location, status action.
Trip must be assigned and in allowed status.
GPS is broadcast only during active trip stage.
System updates trip status and sends real-time location to tracking users.
GPS disabled or invalid status shows warning.
Driver opens passenger list and scans QR or ticks boarding.
Confirm passenger boarding at pickup stops.
Passenger must belong to trip and stop; ticket must be valid.
Boarding can be manual or QR scan.
Invalid QR, wrong trip, or already boarded shows error.
Driver clicks Report Incident during trip.
Report traffic, breakdown, accident, weather, or other incident.
Incident category, optional description/photos, GPS location.
Category is required; description/photos follow limits.
Incident does not automatically change trip status.
System records incident and alerts operator.
Upload failure or missing required field blocks submission.
System lists completed and past assigned trips.
Empty state shown if no history exists.
View assigned trip, schedule, stops and operation details.
Assistant must be assigned to trip.
System displays trip overview and operation tasks.
Assistant opens stop monitoring screen during trip.
Monitor stop arrival and related tasks.
Trip must be active or assigned.
System shows upcoming stop, ETA, passenger and parcel tasks.
Confirm passenger boarding manually or by QR scan.
Ticket must match trip and stop.
Assistant can help driver confirm boarding.
System updates boarding status and highlights pending passengers.
Invalid QR or wrong stop shows error.
Parcel must be assigned to trip and ready for loading.
Loaded parcel moves to next operational status.
Wrong parcel or rejected parcel cannot be loaded.
3.8.5 Manage Parcel Unloading and Delivery Confirmation
Confirm parcel unloading and handover actions.
Parcel must be in transit and at correct drop-off stop.
Delivery confirmation may require recipient action.
System updates unloading and sends confirmation link if needed.
Wrong stop or invalid parcel status blocks action.
Handle parcel no-show, pending transfer, rejection or delivery issue.
Exception action must be allowed for current parcel status.
System records exception and alerts operator if needed.
Operator Staff opens route/station management in dashboard.
Manage station links, routes, stops, stop order, return and alternative routes.
Required fields, valid order, and no conflicting route rules.
Operator can manage only own scope.
System creates/updates route data and validates route consistency.
Duplicate or invalid route/stop data returns error.
Operator Staff creates or updates trip schedule/driver assignment.
Create, update, and manage trip schedules and assignments.
Route, vehicle, driver, assistant, departure time, recurrence if any.
Departure time, vehicle and staff availability must be valid.
Subscription limits may restrict new trips.
System saves schedule and may generate trips.
Conflict, limit exceeded, or invalid time blocks save.
Monitor bookings and booking details under operator scope.
Operator can view own bookings only.
Booking modification by operator is limited to monitoring unless allowed by policy.
System displays booking list, status and passenger contact where permitted.
Operator Staff opens parcel operation dashboard.
Monitor parcel requests and operational cases.
Operator can view own parcels only.
System displays parcel list, statuses and pending actions.
Operator Staff opens extra-large parcel review queue.
Approve, reject or price extra-large parcel requests.
Parcel request details and manual fee/reason.
Review decision is required; fee is required if approved.
System updates parcel review result and notifies sender.
Missing fee/reason or stale request blocks action.
Operator Staff opens live fleet dashboard.
Monitor active trips, vehicle locations, delays, incidents and alerts.
Operator can only monitor own fleet.
Tracking data depends on driver GPS broadcast.
System displays real-time map and operation alerts.
Missing GPS shows last known location or warning.
Operator Staff opens disruption management for an affected trip.
Handle delays, incidents, route changes, vehicle substitution, cancellation and passenger impacts.
Trip ID, disruption type, action, message.
Action must be allowed by trip status and impact rules.
Passenger notifications are sent for impacted bookings.
System applies selected disruption action and records audit log.
Invalid status or missing required reason blocks action.
Operator Staff opens shuttle dispatch or monitoring.
Dispatch or monitor shuttle support for eligible pickups.
Trip, pickup station, shuttle request/passenger info.
Shuttle must be enabled and station/trip must support it.
Only eligible bookings receive shuttle support.
System records dispatch and updates passenger notification.
Unsupported station or disabled module blocks action.
Update company profile, contact details, operated stations and policies.
Required fields and valid policy ranges.
System saves profile/policy and records audit log.
Invalid policy or missing required field returns error.
Create and manage driver, assistant, staff and admin accounts.
Email must be valid and unique; role must be allowed.
New staff sets initial password via invitation link.
System creates/updates personnel accounts and sends invitation when needed.
Duplicate email or limit exceeded blocks creation.
Manage vehicles, types, status and seat layouts.
Vehicle information, vehicle type, seat layout, status.
License/vehicle info must be valid; seat layout must match total seats.
Vehicle must belong to operator and respect subscription limits.
System saves vehicle and seat layout.
Invalid layout, duplicate vehicle, or limit exceeded returns error.
3.10.4 Manage Cargo Capacity and Seat Availability
Operator Admin opens vehicle/trip capacity settings.
Configure cargo capacity and manually disable/enable seats when required.
Capacity must be non-negative; seat action requires valid reason.
Seat disable is manual control and does not create booking.
System updates capacity or seat availability and logs action.
Booked seat or invalid trip status may block changes.
View or manage subscription plan, usage limits and invoices.
Current plan, usage, invoices, upgrade action.
Actions depend on available plans and payment status.
Plan limits affect vehicles, users, routes, trips and modules.
System displays usage and processes upgrade/renewal actions.
Payment failure or expired plan restricts actions.
View wallet balance, pending revenue, settlement history and transactions.
Operator can view own wallet/settlement only.
Settlement follows hold and payout rules.
System displays balance, pending settlements and transaction history.
Operator Admin opens voucher consent page.
Accept or reject operator-funded voucher campaigns.
Only targeted and active proposals can be responded to.
Accepted consent applies to future eligible bookings only.
System records consent and updates voucher applicability.
Expired or already responded proposal blocks duplicate action.
3.10.8 View Operator Dashboard and Export Reports
Operator Admin opens dashboard or report export.
View operational statistics, revenue reports and export supported reports.
Date range and report type must be valid.
System displays dashboard and exports report file when requested.
Large export or invalid filters returns message/retry option.
Create, approve, reject, suspend, update or delete bus operators.
Required company fields and decision reason where needed.
Approval activates operator access and subscription; rejection cancels pending subscription.
System updates operator status and sends notification.
Duplicate company data or invalid status transition is blocked.
System Admin opens user management/activity logs.
Manage accounts, lock/unlock users and review activity logs.
User must exist; action must be allowed.
System updates account status and displays logs.
Invalid action or protected account returns error.
System Admin opens platform station management.
Normalize station data, merge duplicates, update or deactivate invalid stations.
Merge target must be valid; deactivation must be allowed.
Station normalization affects linked operator stations.
System updates station records and re-links mappings when merging.
Invalid merge or active dependency conflict shows warning.
Create and manage platform voucher campaigns and monitor operator consent.
Voucher code, type, value, validity, targets, funding type.
Voucher values, dates and usage limits must be valid.
Operator-funded vouchers require consent before applying.
System creates/updates vouchers and displays adoption/usage status.
Duplicate code or invalid rule blocks save.
System Admin opens subscription plan settings.
Create and manage subscription plans and default plan settings.
Plan name, price, limits, module flags, active status.
Limits and price must be valid; default plan must be active.
Plan changes affect future subscription behavior.
System saves plan and makes it available as configured.
Invalid default or plan used in active subscription may restrict deletion.
System Admin opens platform settlement/wallet console.
Review platform wallet transactions and settlement information across operators.
Date range, operator filter, settlement status.
Data is platform-wide and read/audit oriented.
System displays transactions, settlement statuses and manual settlement action where allowed.
Invalid manual settlement or non-eligible record is blocked.
3.11.7 Manage RAG Knowledge Base and Conversation Audit
Manage RAG documents and review AI conversation logs.
Document file/metadata, approval decision, audit filters.
File type and size must be supported; decision required.
Approved documents can be ingested into RAG knowledge base.
System updates document status and displays conversations for audit.
Unsupported file, failed ingestion or unauthorized access shows error.
Configure platform-level settings and operation parameters.
Changes affect platform behavior and should be audited.
System saves settings and applies them as configured.
Invalid value or protected setting blocks update.
View aggregate platform reports, revenue, operator performance and activity.
Admin role required; filters must be valid.
System displays charts/tables and export option if supported.
View notifications, alerts and operational updates relevant to role.
User can only view own role-relevant notifications.
System displays notification list and statuses.
Navigate to related ticket, trip, parcel, booking, incident or detail screen.
Notification ID and related entity reference.
Notification must belong to user; related entity must be accessible.
Some notifications may be informational only.
System opens related detail screen or displays message.
System dispatches push notification after supported event.
Notify users about booking, trip, parcel, incident, subscription or operation events.
Delivery is asynchronous and may retry.
System sends push and stores in-app notification where required.
Invalid token or provider failure is retried/logged.
User opens AI assistant and submits a question.
Provide role-based answers from RAG knowledge base.
Question cannot be empty; role permission controls accessible knowledge.
AI answers are support information, not system transactions.
System retrieves relevant knowledge and returns an answer.
No relevant data or provider failure shows fallback message.
View previous RAG assistant conversations when available.
User can only view allowed conversation history.
History availability depends on retention policy.
System Admin opens RAG knowledge base management.
Upload, approve, reject, archive and manage RAG documents.
Only approved documents are ingested for retrieval.
System saves document and triggers ingestion after approval.
System Admin opens AI conversation audit logs.
Review AI conversations for monitoring and quality control.
Audit view should respect privacy/security constraints.
System displays conversation logs and metadata.
No matching audit logs shows empty state.
Expire unpaid bookings after payment timeout and release seats.
Booking must remain unpaid past timeout.
Seat release prevents long-term seat holding.
System marks booking expired and releases seats.
Job failure is logged and retried.
Mark pending payment records as expired when confirmation is not received.
Pending payment records and timeout configuration.
Payment must exceed allowed waiting time.
Expired payment cannot later confirm booking without reconciliation.
System updates payment status and notifies related service if needed.
Late gateway callback is handled by idempotency/reconciliation rules.
Scheduled job or schedule creation trigger runs.
Generate trips from driver schedule assignments.
Driver schedule assignment, route, vehicle, time pattern.
Schedule must be active and conflict-free; subscription limits apply.
Generation may be skipped if conflicts or limits occur.
System creates future trips according to schedule.
Conflicts are logged and operator is notified where needed.
Trip time, current status and configured timing rules.
Automation does not override manual exceptional handling unless configured.
System updates trip status such as boarding/completed fallback.
Invalid transition is skipped and logged.
Dispatch push or in-app notifications asynchronously.
Payload and recipient mapping must be valid.
System sends notification and stores record where required.
Failed delivery is retried or logged.
Scheduled job or event consumer evaluates parcel status.
Update parcel statuses when timeout or operation conditions are reached.
Only allowed status transitions are applied.
System updates status and publishes follow-up notifications.
Scheduled job checks subscription periods and usage limits.
Send expiry warnings and mark expired subscriptions.
Expired subscription may restrict operator actions.
System sends warnings and updates subscription status when needed.
Job errors are logged and retried.
Process weekly settlement to operator wallets.
Eligible settlement records and ledger amounts.
Settlement must be eligible and net amount positive.
Funds are held for dispute window before payout.
System settles wallet and records transactions.
Zero/negative settlement is cancelled or skipped.
Extract content, generate embeddings and update RAG knowledge base.
Document must be approved and supported.
Failed ingestion should not make invalid knowledge available.
System extracts chunks, embeds and stores searchable knowledge.
Failed processing is logged and can be retried.
Endpoint should be accessible to monitoring according to config.
Used for uptime and deployment verification.
Failure indicates service issue for alerting.
